summary: Forge now ships the artifact-driven TDD/SDD rollout as the canonical medium-risk implementation path.
Public GitHub visibility guidance must live as tracked release documentation and be covered by release-hardening tests.
revisit_if: The public release flow moves away from GitHub or the checklist becomes fully automated.

summary: Forge 1.11.0 must treat fresh dist-bundle verification and workflow-state round-tripping as release-blocking contracts, not best-effort checks.
revisit_if: The release pipeline stops materializing in-repo dist bundles or help and next no longer consume workflow-state as the durable source of truth.

summary: Forge 1.12.0 should ship with one canonical four-tier release contract plus a separate roadmap-complete closure sweep for the later maturation commits.
revisit_if: A future release changes the canonical tier behavior bar or collapses the roadmap-complete sweep back into the 1.12.0 cut.
resume_hint: A future release changes the canonical tier behavior bar or collapses the roadmap-complete sweep back into the 1.12.0 cut.

summary: Record 1.12.0 as the stable Forge release for its slice because both the canonical release gate and the roadmap-complete closure sweep were green.
evidence: python -m pytest tests/test_release_hardening.py tests/release_repo_test_contracts.py -q -> 26 passed, 82 subtests passed
README.md and docs/release declared 1.12.0 as the stable release at the time
revisit_if: Release archaeology needs the original 1.12.0 proof chain or later evidence invalidates the recorded release verification.
resume_hint: Resume only if a later audit needs the original 1.12.0 proof chain or invalidates the recorded verification.

summary: Record 1.13.0 as the stable Forge release for its slice because the build-packet enhancement line and the canonical repo verification gate were both green.

resume_hint: Resume only if a later audit needs the original 1.13.0 proof chain or invalidates the recorded verification.

summary: Close Forge after 1.15.0 into maintenance mode and treat older roadmap lines plus superseded stable-line decisions as historical context, not active work queues.
revisit_if: A later change needs more than bounded maintenance work under the 1.15.x closure criteria.
resume_hint: Resume only if target-state reopen criteria are met and Forge needs a new explicit roadmap line.

summary: Keep Forge BUILD routing flat across risk levels: Brainstorm, Plan, Architect, Build, and Review own readiness/proof strength, while the old pre-build spec-review fork is removed from active routing.
evidence: python -m unittest discover -s packages/forge-core/tests -v -> PASS on 2026-04-21
next: Decide whether to bump the release version and push/merge to main.
revisit_if: Future routing reintroduces a separate pre-build fork or legacy readiness artifacts stop bootstrapping into the flat build chain.
resume_hint: Resume from the flat routing plan and verify registry, route preview, workflow-state bootstrap, Codex overlay, and Antigravity overlay together.
